seoTitle: How to Restore a Deleted Microsoft Loop Page
metaDescription: Recover an accidentally deleted Microsoft Loop page using the workspace trash bin, OneDrive, or SharePoint recycle bin recovery methods.
intro: Accidentally deleting a Loop page doesn't have to mean losing your work for good. This guide walks through the different places a deleted page can be recovered from, including the workspace's own recycle bin, your personal OneDrive, and connected SharePoint channels.

steps.heading: Open the workspace trash bin
steps.body: Open your target workspace and scroll to the very bottom of the left sidebar to find the recycle bin icon.

steps.heading: Find and restore the page
steps.body: Click the recycle bin icon to reveal disposed pages, hover over the missing title, click the three dots icon, and select Restore to push it back into your sidebar.

steps.heading: Check your OneDrive trash for individual files
steps.body: If the page was private or tied to a personal chat, open your Microsoft 365 OneDrive hub and check its trash drawer for the missing Loop file.

steps.heading: Check SharePoint for shared channel assets
steps.body: For pages shared through a Teams channel, open the connected Files tab and check SharePoint's recycle bin to recover the component.

steps.heading: Escalate to an admin if needed
steps.body: If a page was permanently removed or an entire workspace was deleted, your IT admin may still be able to recover it through the admin dashboard within the retention window.

faq.question: How long do deleted Loop pages stay recoverable?
faq.answer: Deleted items typically stay in the recycle bin for a limited retention window, so it's best to restore a page as soon as you notice it's missing.

faq.question: Can I recover a page if the whole workspace was deleted?
faq.answer: In more serious cases, an IT admin may be able to use admin tools to recover a fully deleted workspace before the retention window closes.

faq.question: Where do private Loop pages get stored when deleted?
faq.answer: Pages tied to individual chats are generally recoverable through your personal OneDrive trash rather than the shared workspace bin.

faq.question: Will restoring a page bring back its comments and tables?
faq.answer: Restoring a page from the recycle bin generally returns it to its last saved state, including its structured components, though it's worth double-checking after recovery.
